creator: crest@rlwinm.de
text: This feature is especially useful if you don't want to go through a shell because it saves the complexity and overhead of something as like `jexec -- "$jail" "sh -c 'cd \"$1\" && shift && exec \"$@\"' "$dir" "$@"`. Btw the lang/execline port contains an cd executable that supports chainloading e.g. `execline-cd "$dir" "$@"`. Which at least avoids half of the annoyance at the cost of installing a package.
